ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Na n’egya Isaak bisaa no dɛ, Woana nye wo? Na ɔsee no dɛ, Mara wo ba, w’abakan Esau a.
translated: And his father Isaac asked him, Who are you? And he said, I am your firstborn Esau.

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei ma w’enyiwa do, na hwɛ, mpɔnkyenyin a wɔfow mpɔnkyeber no nyinara hɔn ho yɛ nsensanee nsensanee, woranworan, nye ntokowa ntokowa: na mehu dza Laban reyɛ wo nyinara.
translated: And he said, Now behold, I have taken upon me to speak unto the Lord, and I said, Peradventure there shall be twenty found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for twenty's sake.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Nna akyɛdze no twaa mu dzii n’enyim: na ɔnoara dze, ɔtsenaa dɔm no mu anafua no. Jakob N’aperdzi Wɔ Peniel
translated: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two womenservants, and his eleven sons, and passed over the ford Jabbok.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.
translated: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and where you made a vow to me. Now leave this land at once and go back to your native land.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Na Jakob see Laban dɛ, Fa me yer ma me, na me nda aso, ma monkɔ ne nkyɛn.
translated: And Jacob said to Laban, Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in unto her.
